items.title: Trump takes aim at MAGA supporters calling for release of Epstein documents | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The US President Donald Trump has filed a billion-dollar lawsuit against the Wall Street Journal and its owner Rupert Murdoch. The newspaper had claimed that Donald Trump wrote a lewd birthday message to the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.  Trump is under intense pressure from his usually loyal supporters for a lack of transparency around the Epstein case. In response, the president has asked a federal court to unseal grand jury testimony from the prosecution against the disgraced financier. Janelle Dumalaon, DW's correspondent in Washington D.C. has more

#epsteincase #epsteinfiles #trump

items.title: How different is the latest DR Congo ceasefire from previous peace deals that failed? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The Democratic Republic of Congo and the M23 rebel group have agreed in principle to end fighting in eastern Congo. The United States has been pressing for a lasting peace deal during months of talks in Qatar. The fighting has killed thousands this year in a conflict dating back more than three decades.

00:00 US and Qatar broker ceasefire deal between DR Congo and M23 rebels
02:32 Kambale Musavuli, international human rights advocate from the DRC

items.title: Car rams into crowd in LA | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Police say a driver who rammed a car in to a crowd waiting to enter a night club in Los Angeles had been shot.  It's not  clear whether the driver was shot before or after he drove into the crowd. 30 people were injured in the incident in East Hollywood.

items.title: Why Syria's Interim President is not talking retaliation after Israeli strikes this week | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Syrian interim president Ahmed al-Sharaa has announced an immediate ceasefire in the Sweida province after clashes between Syrian government forces and rival Bedouin and Druze fighters. A war monitor says the death toll from a week of violence in the area south of Damascus has risen to 940 people. Tens of thousands of people have been displaced.

items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Hundreds of men accused of belonging to a Venezuelan criminal gang have been released from a high security prison in El Salvador. Now they have been sent to Venezuela in a prisoner swap between Caracas and Washington. Ten Americans held by Caracas were also released in the exchange.

items.title: Cameroon's 92-year-old President Paul Biya seeks 8th term | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Cameroon's 92-year-old President Paul Biya has announced he will seek an eighth term in power. Elections are scheduled for October. Biya's had frequent health problems and spends much of his time abroad. Here is a look at Africa's second longest serving president, who first came to power in 1982.

00:00 Paul Biya seeks 8th term in Cameroon
04:57 DW speaks with Agbor Balla - President of the Centre for Human Rights and Democracy in Africa and Founder of the youtube channel 'Agbor Balla Speaks'

items.title: What Israel's attacks mean for Syria's new government | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Israel and Syria have agreed to a ceasefire after Israel's bombardment of Damascus this week. That's according to Washington's special envoy for Syria Tom Barrack. The reported deal follows renewed clashes between Syrian government forces and rival Bedouin and Druze fighters. Israel said it intervened to protect the minority Druze community. The recent violence has killed 718 people and displaced tens of thousands. The UN says it has credible reports of summary executions and arbitrary killings.

00:00 Israel, Syria agree to ceasefire
00:40 DW's Aya Ibrahim reports from Beirut
05:12 DW speaks with Fawaz Gerges, Professor of Middle Eastern Politics and International Relations at the London School of Economics

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Syrian leader says army deploying to Sweida following ceasefire collapse | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Reports state that the Syrian army will redeploy to Sweida, after the collapse of a ceasefire deal. 

Friday saw a fresh outbreak of violence between Sunni Bedouin militia and Druze fighters. Under a US-brokered deal, Syrian government forces had withdrawn from the area on Thursday, after coming under heavy bombardment by Israel, which supports the Druze religious minority. 

The violence of recent days has left almost 600 people dead. The UN's human rights agency, the OHCHR, is calling for the killing to stop and for those responsible to be held to account. 

Chapters:
0:00 Syrian army redeploys to south
1:10 Caroline Rose, Middle East analyst
8:34 Joost Hiltermann, International Crisis Group
14:04 Robert E. Hunter,, Middle East expert

items.title: Brazil's Bolsonaro banned from using social media, ordered to wear monitoring device | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Brazil's Supreme Court has banned former president Jair Bolsonaro from using social media, and ordered him to wear an electronic monitoring device, as he awaits the verdict in his trial.

Bolsonaro called the measures politically motivated and humiliating, and rejected fears he was planning to flee abroad. He is fighting charges of plotting to overthrow the government of his successor Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva following his election defeat in 2022.  The trial has prompted a tariff feud with the US. 

Chapters:
0:00 Bolsonaro ordered to wear device
0:34 Sam Cowie, Journalist

items.title: Germany says it has deported dozens of law-breaking Afghans | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Germany says it has deported dozens of Afghans, convicted of crimes, to their home country, the first time under Chancellor Friedrich Merz's new government. The deportations came as Germany hosted a summit with EU partners focusing on migration. Germany's interior minister Alexander Dobrindt said that Germany wanted to take a leading role in the EU's migration policy.

items.title: US Congress approves spending cuts slashing public media | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>We start in the United States, where Congress has given final approval to Donald Trump's request to claw back nine-billion dollars of government spending. The so-called "rescission package" cancels funding that lawmakers had already allocated.  

The bill passed narrowly last night in the House of Representatives, with only two Republicans joining Democrats to oppose it. Most of the cuts target foreign aid approved before Trump returned to office -- programs the White House now describes as "waste, fraud and abuse". Democrats say the aid freeze is reckless and will impact global health and cost lives in the world's poorest nations.

The legislation also slashes more than a billion dollars for American public media, which US conservatives have long accused of being biased against them. 

Donald Trump whipped Republicans into line last week, posting on social media: â€śIt is very important that all Republicans adhere to my Recissions Bill and, in particular, defund the Corporation for Public Broadcasting (PBS and NPR)... Any Republican that votes to allow this monstrosity to continue broadcasting will not have my support or endorsement.â€ť  

Chapters:
0:00 Congress approves Trump cuts
0:40 Public media cuts
2:14 Ryan Howlett, Public broadcasting fundraiser

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Tesla opens first showroom in India | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>After years of delays, US electric vehicle maker Tesla has made its long-anticipated debut in India, the world's third-biggest auto market. 

Tesla opened its first showroom in Mumbai this week, with plans for another in Delhi. Spearheading Tesla's line-up is the Y Model, retailing in India for upwards of 70,000 dollars. Sales of Tesla EVs have fallen sharply this year, as buyers around the world boycott the company over the political views of its CEO Elon Musk. 

Chapters:
0:00 Tesla enters India
0:33 Peter Wells, Cardiff Business School
